title: Severe chest pain in a 22-year-old with sickle cell disease
presentation: A 22-year-old male with known sickle cell disease presents with severe bilateral chest pain, fever, and worsening shortness of breath over 24 hours. He had a vaso-occlusive crisis 3 days ago treated at home. Vitals: BP 128/82, HR 118, RR 28, Temp 38.9C, SpO2 88% on room air. Exam: decreased breath sounds bilaterally, dullness to percussion at bases, new infiltrates visible.

gold_standard_workup.key_decision_point: New pulmonary infiltrate + fever + chest pain + hypoxia in sickle cell patient = acute chest syndrome. Most dangerous complication — mortality up to 3%. Exchange transfusion for rapidly deteriorating patients. Do NOT over-hydrate.
